intro: 86 AI items this week (50 India-relevant) across 9 sources.

items.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
items.summary: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

items.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
items.summary: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

items.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
items.summary: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

items.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
items.summary: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work ever…

items.title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
items.summary: Google's 24/7 agentic assistant, Gemini Spark, comes to Mac alongside other improvements, like real-time tracking and support for more apps.

items.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
items.summary: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Sci…

items.title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
items.summary: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety ar…

items.title: Hugging Face and Cerebras bring Gemma 4 to real-time voice AI
ScarfBench: Benchmarking AI Agents for Enterprise Java Framework Migration
AI agents are not your “coworkers”
items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Imagine coming in to work to learn that a new underling will report to you. The worker is not a person but an AI tool—one that your company…

items.title: Agent confidence on the technical frontier
items.summary: Enterprise investment in AI is booming. Gartner is calling 2026 an “inflection year” for organizations to align their AI projects with strategic business objectives. As the pressure to prove ROI mounts, executives and technology leaders are looking to agentic AI to drive the m…

items.title: Featuring Every Eval Ever Results on Hugging Face Model Pages
New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
items.summary: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

items.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
items.summary: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

items.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
items.summary: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

items.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
items.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
items.summary: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

items.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
items.summary: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

items.title: AI investment in emerging markets must go beyond models to ecosystems: Report
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is poised to revolutionize economies, but emerging markets must cultivate local ecosystems to truly benefit, a World Bank report reveals. Beyond importing models, success hinges on robust digital infrastructure, skilled talent, and adaptable AI building…

items.title: MoS Kirti Vardhan Singh to lead India delegation at first UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance
items.summary: India's Minister of State for External Affairs, Kirti Vardhan Singh, is leading a delegation to Geneva for the inaugural UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance. This crucial forum will review the first independent scientific assessment of AI's capabilities, risks, and opportuniti…

items.title: Microsoft creates new business unit to help large enterprises set up tailored AI solutions
AI capex cycle likely to end with market pushback, not spending cuts: Jefferies
items.summary: Global brokerage Jefferies suggests the AI investment boom might falter not due to tech giants cutting spending, but investor impatience for returns. A significant wealth transfer to North Asia, evident in surging Korean and Taiwanese market caps, highlights where AI capital i…

items.title: For one small business, AI was key to a quick start and expansion
IT services growth likely to stay soft in near term as clients delay discretionary spends: Equirus
items.summary: India's major IT firms anticipate a subdued first quarter, with growth expected to pick up only when clients shift from cost-cutting to AI-driven transformations. Enterprises remain cautious with spending due to global uncertainties, funding AI through existing savings. While …

items.title: Platforms, segmentation, AI: Test your business creativity with Edition 239 of our weekly quiz!
items.summary: This insightful feature from YourStory tests and strengthens your business acumen! Here are 5 questions to kick off this 239th quiz. Ready?

items.title: Orbital plans space data centres to power AI, seeks FCC clearance for 100,000 satellites
items.summary: A US startup, Orbital, is looking to set up space-based data centres to meet the surging demand for AI computing power. The company aims to launch its first data centre satellite next year, with plans for 100,000 satellites in low Earth orbit to deliver 10 gigawatts of compute…

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.

repos.name: ai-weekly-report
repos.desc: My personalized AI newsletter that runs on an agentic AI
